name: Denver Gold Shop
description_short: Colorado's largest rare coin and precious metals dealer since 1976, buying and selling collectible coins, bullion, currency, jewelry, and Rolex watches from two Denver-area locations.
description_long:
Rocky Mountain Coin was founded in 1976 and has grown to become one of the largest rare coin and precious metals dealers in the United States, handling millions of dollars in transactions annually. The company operates two showroom locations in Denver and Greenwood Village, Colorado, and serves customers nationwide. The business specializes in tangible asset transactions, catering to both collectors and investors seeking liquidity or portfolio diversification through physical precious metals and numismatic items.

Rocky Mountain Coin buys and sells rare coins, numismatic currency, gold and silver bullion, platinum, gold jewelry, and pre-owned Rolex watches. They also serve as a Colorado premier dealer for Garrett and Minelab metal detectors. The company offers appraisals, in-store jewelry sales, and consultations—services not all listed on their website. Their inventory is continuously refreshed, with new stock regularly entering their stores. They maintain competitive daily pricing for both buying and selling transactions.

The company distinguishes itself through deep professional expertise and institutional credibility. Their staff includes highly qualified professional numismatists with combined experience exceeding 150 years. Founder and president Klaus J. Degler has over 40 years of ownership and management experience, has served as president of the Colorado Professional Numismatic Association, and has performed consultations and appraisals for the City of Denver, IRS, Colorado Bureau of Investigation, General Services Administration, and Colorado Museum of Natural History. This level of institutional engagement and professional qualification is uncommon in the pawn and precious metals sector.

Rocky Mountain Coin operates transparently within the collector and investment precious metals market. However, like all businesses in this category, pricing depends on daily commodity markets and the subjective valuation of rare/collectible items. The company's strength lies in serving serious collectors, investors, and individuals with significant precious metal or numismatic holdings rather than consumers seeking quick cash for modest amounts of personal items. Their focus on rare coins and bullion, rather than general pawn transactions, positions them as a specialized dealer rather than a traditional pawn shop.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Founded in 1976 with over 40 years of ownership continuity and deep market expertise
Staff of professional numismatists with combined 150+ years of experience
Founder Klaus Degler has performed consultations for City of Denver, IRS, CBI, GSA, and Colorado Museum of Natural History
Two brick-and-mortar showroom locations with continuously refreshed inventory
Buys and sells nationwide, not limited to local transactions
Offers specialized services including appraisals and in-store jewelry sales beyond basic transactions
Positioned as Colorado's largest precious metals dealer of its type
cons: Pricing is tied to daily commodity markets and subjective collector valuations—not transparent fixed rates
Primary focus on rare coins and bullion may result in lower offers for common or damaged items
Business model caters to serious collectors and investors rather than consumers seeking quick cash for modest personal items
Limited transparency on typical buy/sell margins or pricing methodology on their website
No mention of authentication guarantees or return policies for purchased items
best_for: Collectors of rare coins and currency seeking to liquidate holdings at fair market value
Precious metals investors buying or selling bullion (gold, silver, platinum)
Individuals with significant gold jewelry or pre-owned Rolex watches to sell
People needing professional appraisals for legal, tax, or insurance purposes
diagnosis: Rocky Mountain Coin is best suited for serious collectors, investors, and individuals with substantial rare coin, bullion, or precious metal holdings seeking expert valuation and fair-market transactions. The primary caveat is that this is a specialized dealer, not a traditional pawn shop—pricing depends on daily commodity markets and collector demand, and their expertise commands respect but comes with corresponding expectations of significant transaction values.
services: Buy rare coins at competitive daily prices
Sell rare coins, bullion, and numismatic currency
Gold jewelry purchase and in-store sales
Pre-owned Rolex watch buying and selling
Professional appraisals for coins, currency, and precious metals
Expert consultations for collectors and investors
Garrett and Minelab metal detector sales
Nationwide mail-in buying (in addition to in-person transactions)
Liquidation services for collections of rare coins and currency
Educational content and guides on coin collecting and precious metal investment
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 250 W Jewell Ave UNIT B, Denver, CO 80223
